verses.text: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work: But the seventh day is the sabbath of the LORD thy God: in it thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates:
verses.note: Sabbath rhythm built into work

verses.text: It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, to eat the bread of sorrows: for so he giveth his beloved sleep.
verses.note: Rest as trust in God's provision

title: Money & Finances
description: Biblical wisdom on wealth, debt, saving, and the heart's relationship to material possessions.

verses.text: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
verses.note: Money reveals heart allegiance

verses.text: For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
verses.note: The love of money — not money itself — is the root

verses.text: No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
verses.note: Binary loyalty — God or wealth

verses.text: Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
verses.note: God's presence outweighs possessions

verses.text: Remove far from me vanity and lies: give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me: Lest I be full, and deny thee, and say, Who is the LORD? or lest I be poor, and steal, and take the name of my God in vain.
verses.note: Agur's prayer — the middle way

verses.text: There is treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up.
verses.note: Saving as wisdom

verses.text: He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much. If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?
verses.note: Faithfulness with small things qualifies for greater responsibility

verses.text: Owe no man any thing, but to love one another: for he that loveth another hath fulfilled the law.
verses.note: Debt avoidance as principle

title: Suffering
description: The reality of pain in a fallen world, its redemptive purposes, and the hope that sustains believers through affliction.

verses.text: And not only so, but we glory in tribulations also: knowing that tribulation worketh patience; And patience, experience; and experience, hope:
verses.note: The progressive chain — tribulation to hope

verses.text: My brethren, count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations; Knowing this, that the trying of your faith worketh patience.
verses.note: Joy in trials because of what they produce

verses.text: Wherein ye greatly rejoice, though now for a season, if need be, ye are in heaviness through manifold temptations: That the trial of your faith, being much more precious than of gold that perisheth, though it be tried with fire, might be found unto praise and honour and glory at the appearing of Jesus Christ:
verses.note: Faith refined like gold

verses.text: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
verses.note: Through the valley — not around it

verses.text: When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee; and through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee: when thou walkest through the fire, thou shalt not be burned; neither shall the flame kindle upon thee.
verses.note: Divine accompaniment through every trial

verses.text: Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort; Who comforteth us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.
verses.note: Comfort received becomes comfort given

verses.text: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief: and we hid as it were our faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not.
verses.note: The Servant knows suffering firsthand

verses.text: For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin.
verses.note: Christ's empathy born of experience

verses.text: For even hereunto were ye called: because Christ also suffered for us, leaving us an example, that ye should follow his steps:
verses.note: His suffering as our pattern

title: Decision-Making
description: Seeking God's will through Scripture, counsel, prayer, and wisdom when facing choices.

verses.text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
verses.note: The foundational text on divine guidance

verses.text: Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
verses.note: Commitment precedes clarity

verses.text: O LORD, I know that the way of man is not in himself: it is not in man that walketh to direct his steps.
verses.note: Human limitation in self-guidance

verses.text: Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established.
verses.note: Wise counsel stabilizes plans

verses.text: Where no counsel is, the people fall: but in the multitude of counsellors there is safety.
verses.note: Safety in many advisers

verses.text: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.
verses.note: Teachability as long-term investment

verses.text: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
verses.note: Scripture illuminates the next step

verses.text: The entrance of thy words giveth light; it giveth understanding unto the simple.
verses.note: Understanding through the word

verses.text: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.
verses.note: God gives wisdom generously to those who ask

title: Rest
description: Physical, spiritual, and eschatological rest — from Sabbath rhythm to the eternal rest promised in Christ.

verses.text: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made. And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.
verses.note: God models rest — not from exhaustion but completion

verses.text: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
verses.note: Commandment to remember — active preservation

verses.text: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
verses.note: Sabbath serves human flourishing

verses.text: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.
verses.note: The great invitation — soul-rest through Christ

verses.text: There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God. For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God did from his.
verses.note: Ceasing from self-effort

verses.text: I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, LORD, only makest me dwell in safety.
verses.note: Peace and safety from God alone

verses.text: It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, to eat the bread of sorrows: for so he giveth his beloved sleep.
verses.note: Sleep as gift — not earned, received

title: Hope
description: Biblical hope as confident expectation rooted in God's character and promises — not wishful thinking but anchored assurance.

verses.text: Which hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and stedfast, and which entereth into that within the veil;
verses.note: Anchor metaphor — hope holds in the storm

verses.text: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.
verses.note: Hope overflows through the Spirit

verses.text: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.
verses.note: God's plans include a future and hope

verses.text: For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for? But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.
verses.note: Unseen hope requires patience

verses.text: It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not. They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
verses.note: New mercies as daily grounds for hope

verses.text: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.
verses.note: Self-counsel to hope despite despair

title: Peace
description: Shalom in its full biblical scope — inner tranquility, reconciled relationships, and the cosmic peace secured by Christ.

verses.text: Therefore being justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ:
verses.note: Justification ends the enmity

verses.text: And, having made peace through the blood of his cross, by him to reconcile all things unto himself; by him, I say, whether they be things in earth, or things in heaven.
verses.note: Cosmic peace through the cross

verses.text: For he is our peace, who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us;
verses.note: Christ himself is our peace

verses.text: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God. And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
verses.note: Peace that surpasses understanding — guards hearts

verses.text: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
verses.note: Christ's peace differs from the world's

verses.text: Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
verses.note: Perfect peace through fixed focus on God

title: Joy
description: Joy as deep-rooted gladness in God that transcends circumstances — a fruit of the Spirit, not a product of ease.

verses.text: Then he said unto them, Go your way, eat the fat, and drink the sweet, and send portions unto them for whom nothing is prepared: for this day is holy unto our Lord: neither be ye sorry; for the joy of the LORD is your strength.
verses.note: Joy as strength — not weakness or frivolity

verses.text: Thou wilt shew me the path of life: in thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore.
verses.note: Fullness of joy in God's presence

verses.text: Although the fig tree shall not blossom, neither shall fruit be in the vines; the labour of the olive shall fail, and the fields shall yield no meat; the flock shall be cut off from the fold, and there shall be no herd in the stalls: Yet I will rejoice in the LORD, I will joy in the God of my salvation.
verses.note: Joy despite total material loss

verses.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,
verses.note: Joy as Spirit-produced fruit

verses.text: Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice.
verses.note: A command repeated — joy is a choice

verses.text: Rejoice evermore.
verses.note: The shortest imperative — always rejoicing

title: Love (Agape)
description: Self-giving, unconditional love modeled by God in Christ — the highest virtue and the fulfillment of the law.

verses.text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
verses.note: The scope and cost of divine love

verses.text: But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
verses.note: Love demonstrated to the undeserving

verses.text: Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins.
verses.note: Love defined by propitiation

verses.text: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up, Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil; Rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth; Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.
verses.note: The attributes of love — fifteen descriptors

verses.text: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another. By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
verses.note: Love as the mark of discipleship

verses.text: Jesus said unto him, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind. This is the first and great commandment. And the second is like unto it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.note: The double love command — vertical and horizontal

verses.text: My little children, let us not love in word, neither in tongue; but in deed and in truth.
verses.note: Love requires action, not just words

verses.text: Let love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good. Be kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love; in honour preferring one another;
verses.note: Genuine love — no hypocrisy

title: Obedience
description: Faithful response to God's commands — not legalism but love-motivated submission to divine authority.

verses.text: And Samuel said, Hath the LORD as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the LORD? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to hearken than the fat of rams.
verses.note: Obedience outranks ritual

verses.text: If ye love me, keep my commandments.
verses.note: Love and obedience inseparable

verses.text: Therefore thou shalt love the LORD thy God, and keep his charge, and his statutes, and his judgments, and his commandments, alway.
verses.note: Love expressed through keeping commandments

verses.text: And it shall come to pass, if thou shalt hearken diligently unto the voice of the LORD thy God, to observe and to do all his commandments which I command thee this day, that the LORD thy God will set thee on high above all nations of the earth: And all these blessings shall come on thee, and overtake thee, if thou shalt hearken unto the voice of the LORD thy God.
verses.note: Blessings conditioned on obedience

verses.text: But whoso looketh into the perfect law of liberty, and continueth therein, he being not a forgetful hearer, but a doer of the work, this man shall be blessed in his deed.
verses.note: Blessing comes to doers, not hearers only

verses.text: If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father's commandments, and abide in his love.
verses.note: Abiding in love through obedience

title: Perseverance
description: Enduring faithfully through difficulty, opposition, and delay — pressing on toward the prize.

verses.text: Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset us, and let us run with patience the race that is set before us, Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.
verses.note: Jesus as the model of endurance

verses.text: Brethren, I count not myself to have apprehended: but this one thing I do, forgetting those things which are behind, and reaching forth unto those things which are before, I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.
verses.note: Forward focus — pressing on

verses.text: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
verses.note: Harvest requires endurance

verses.text: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.
verses.note: Steadfastness because labor matters

verses.text: Blessed is the man that endureth temptation: for when he is tried, he shall receive the crown of life, which the Lord hath promised to them that love him.
verses.note: Crown of life for those who endure

verses.text: I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith:
verses.note: Paul's testimony — fought, finished, kept

title: Resisting Temptation
description: The nature of temptation, its source, and the divine provision for overcoming it.

verses.text: But every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed. Then when lust hath conceived, it bringeth forth sin: and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.
verses.note: Desire → enticement → sin → death

verses.text: For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world.
verses.note: Three categories matching Eden and Christ's temptation

verses.text: And when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make one wise, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband with her; and he did eat.
verses.note: The original template of temptation

verses.text: There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able; but will with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may be able to bear it.
verses.note: No temptation beyond capacity — escape always provided

verses.text: For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted.
verses.note: Christ's experiential help

verses.text: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
verses.note: Submission to God precedes resistance to evil

title: Spiritual Warfare
description: The reality of invisible conflict between spiritual forces of good and evil, and the believer's role in the battle.

verses.text: For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places.
verses.note: The real enemy is not human

verses.text: For though we walk in the flesh, we do not war after the flesh: (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)
verses.note: Spiritual weapons for spiritual battles

verses.text: Be sober, be vigilant; because your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour:
verses.note: The enemy prowls — vigilance required

verses.text: Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.
verses.note: Greater power resides within

verses.text: Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us.
verses.note: More than conquerors — overwhelming victory

verses.text: And having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it.
verses.note: Christ's triumph over hostile powers at the cross

title: The Armor of God
description: The divine equipment described in Ephesians 6 for standing against spiritual opposition — each piece representing a facet of the believer's defense.

verses.text: Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand. Stand therefore, having your loins girt about with truth, and having on the breastplate of righteousness; And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace; Above all, taking the shield of faith, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked. And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:
verses.note: Six pieces — belt, breastplate, shoes, shield, helmet, sword

verses.text: Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
verses.note: Whole armor — no partial equipping

verses.text: Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all perseverance and supplication for all saints;
verses.note: Prayer undergirds every piece of armor

verses.text: And he said, Hearken ye, all Judah, and ye inhabitants of Jerusalem, and thou king Jehoshaphat, Thus saith the LORD unto you, Be not afraid nor dismayed by reason of this great multitude; for the battle is not yours, but God's.
verses.note: The battle belongs to God — prayer acknowledges dependence

title: Trusting God
description: Active reliance on God's character, promises, and sovereignty — especially when circumstances seem contrary.

verses.text: And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou, LORD, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.
verses.note: Knowing God's name produces trust

verses.text: Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
verses.note: Stillness as trust — ceasing striving

verses.text: The LORD is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
verses.note: God as fortress for the trusting

verses.text: What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee. In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
verses.note: Trust as the response to fear — not its absence

verses.text: Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God: I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee; yea, I will uphold thee with the right hand of my righteousness.
verses.note: Three promises — strengthen, help, uphold

verses.text: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe.
verses.note: Fear of man ensnares; trust in God liberates

title: Guidance
description: God's faithful direction of his people through his Word, Spirit, providence, and community.

verses.text: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: he leadeth me beside the still waters. He restoreth my soul: he leadeth me in the paths of righteousness for his name's sake.
verses.note: The Shepherd leads to rest and righteousness

verses.text: I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.
verses.note: Personal instruction and watchful care

verses.text: And the LORD shall guide thee continually, and satisfy thy soul in drought, and make fat thy bones: and thou shalt be like a watered garden, and like a spring of water, whose waters fail not.
verses.note: Continual guidance even in drought

verses.text: Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.
verses.note: The Spirit guides into all truth

verses.text: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
verses.note: Spirit-leading marks sonship

verses.text: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.
verses.note: Living and walking aligned

title: Contentment
description: Satisfaction rooted in God's sufficiency rather than circumstances — learning to be at peace regardless of plenty or want.

verses.text: Not that I speak in respect of want: for I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content. I know both how to be abased, and I know how to abound: every where and in all things I am instructed both to be full and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer need.
verses.note: Contentment is learned, not automatic

verses.text: But godliness with contentment is great gain. For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out. And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.
verses.note: Godliness plus contentment — the real wealth

verses.text: Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
verses.note: God's presence as the basis for contentment

verses.text: Whom have I in heaven but thee? and there is none upon earth that I desire beside thee. My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
verses.note: God as the ultimate portion

verses.text: And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
verses.note: Sufficiency in grace, not circumstance

title: Generosity & Giving
description: The call to cheerful, sacrificial giving — reflecting God's own generosity and trusting him to provide.

verses.text: But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully. Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver.
verses.note: Sowing and reaping principle — cheerfulness required

verses.text: I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.
verses.note: Jesus' unrecorded saying — giving outranks receiving

verses.text: The liberal soul shall be made fat: and he that watereth shall be watered also himself.
verses.note: Generosity returns to the giver

verses.text: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the LORD; and that which he hath given will he pay him again.
verses.note: Giving to the poor is lending to God

verses.text: And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me.
verses.note: Service to the least is service to Christ

verses.text: Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.
verses.note: Overflowing return — the measure you give is the measure you receive

title: Hospitality
description: Welcoming strangers and sharing resources as a reflection of God's welcome — love made tangible through open doors and tables.

verses.text: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
verses.note: Abraham's example echoed — angels at the table

verses.text: Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to hospitality.
verses.note: Hospitality as a practice, not an afterthought

verses.text: Use hospitality one to another without grudging.
verses.note: Without grumbling — cheerful welcome

verses.text: And he lift up his eyes and looked, and, lo, three men stood by him: and when he saw them, he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself toward the ground, And said, My Lord, if now I have found favour in thy sight, pass not away, I pray thee, from thy servant: Let a little water, I pray you, be fetched, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree: And I will fetch a morsel of bread, and comfort ye your hearts; after that ye shall pass on: for therefore are ye come to your servant. And they said, So do, as thou hast said.
verses.note: Abraham runs to serve — lavish welcome

verses.text: And it fell on a day, that Elisha passed to Shunem, where was a great woman; and she constrained him to eat bread. And so it was, that as oft as he passed by, he turned in thither to eat bread. And she said unto her husband, Behold now, I perceive that this is an holy man of God, which passeth by us continually. Let us make a little chamber, I pray thee, on the wall; and let us set for him there a bed, and a table, and a stool, and a candlestick: and it shall be, when he cometh to us, that he shall turn in thither.
verses.note: The Shunammite woman — creating space for a prophet

title: Speech & Words
description: The power of the tongue for blessing or destruction — guarding speech, speaking truth, and the weight of words before God.

verses.text: Death and life are in the power of the tongue: and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.
verses.note: Binary power — death or life

verses.text: Even so the tongue is a little member, and boasteth great things. Behold, how great a matter a little fire kindleth! And the tongue is a fire, a world of iniquity: so is the tongue among our members, that it defileth the whole body, and setteth on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire of hell.
verses.note: Small but devastating — fire metaphor

verses.text: A wholesome tongue is a tree of life: but perverseness therein is a breach in the spirit.
verses.note: Healing or wounding — no neutral speech

verses.text: Set a watch, O LORD, before my mouth; keep the door of my lips.
verses.note: Prayer for divine restraint on speech

verses.text: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.
verses.note: Speech should build up and give grace

verses.text: Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.
verses.note: Grace-seasoned speech — winsome and truthful

verses.text: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
verses.note: Accountability for every careless word

title: The Thought Life
description: Guarding the mind and setting it on things above — the inner world where spiritual battles are first won or lost.

verses.text: Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are honest, whatsoever things are just, whatsoever things are pure, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report; if there be any virtue, and if there be any praise, think on these things.
verses.note: Eight filters for thought life

verses.text: Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ;
verses.note: Active warfare — taking thoughts captive

verses.text: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
verses.note: Heart-guarding as life's priority

verses.text: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
verses.note: Transformation through mental renewal

verses.text: Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.
verses.note: Deliberate orientation toward heavenly realities

verses.text: Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
verses.note: Fixed mind produces perfect peace

title: Time & Seasons
description: The biblical understanding of time as purposeful and God-ordained — seasons of life, kairos moments, and redeeming the time.

verses.text: To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven: A time to be born, and a time to die; a time to plant, and a time to pluck up that which is planted;
verses.note: Sovereignty over every season of life

verses.text: And he said unto them, It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.
verses.note: Times and seasons under divine authority

verses.text: My times are in thy hand: deliver me from the hand of mine enemies, and from them that persecute me.
verses.note: Personal times held in God's hand

verses.text: Redeeming the time, because the days are evil.
verses.note: Making the most of every opportunity

verses.text: So teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.
verses.note: Numbering days produces wisdom

verses.text: Whereas ye know not what shall be on the morrow. For what is your life? It is even a vapour, that appeareth for a little time, and then vanisheth away.
verses.note: Life's brevity as motivation

title: Death & Dying
description: The biblical view of death as the last enemy conquered by Christ — from mourning to hope.

verses.text: O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory? The sting of death is sin; and the strength of sin is the law. But thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.note: Death's sting removed through Christ

verses.text: But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:
verses.note: Death abolished — life and immortality revealed

verses.text: And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
verses.note: The final eradication of death

verses.text: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
verses.note: Paul's calculus — death as gain

verses.text: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
verses.note: No fear in the valley because of the Shepherd

verses.text: We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
verses.note: Absent from body equals present with Christ

title: Grief
description: Mourning loss with honesty before God while holding to the hope of resurrection and restoration.

verses.text: A time to weep, and a time to laugh; a time to mourn, and a time to dance;
verses.note: Grief has its appointed season

verses.text: Jesus wept.
verses.note: The shortest verse — Christ weeps at death

verses.text: The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
verses.note: God draws near to the brokenhearted

verses.text: But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as others which have no hope.
verses.note: Grief with hope — distinct from hopeless sorrow

verses.text: For his anger endureth but a moment; in his favour is life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
verses.note: Night of weeping gives way to morning joy

verses.text: For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.
verses.note: God himself wipes away every tear

title: Healing
description: God as healer of body and soul — from physical miracles to the restoration of the whole person.

verses.text: And said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the LORD thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his sight, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of these diseases upon thee, which I have brought upon the Egyptians: for I am the LORD that healeth thee.
verses.note: Yahweh Rapha — I am the LORD who heals you

verses.text: Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits: Who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases;
verses.note: Forgiveness and healing paired

verses.text: Heal me, O LORD, and I shall be healed; save me, and I shall be saved: for thou art my praise.
verses.note: God's healing is complete and certain

verses.text: But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.
verses.note: Healing through substitutionary suffering

verses.text: When the even was come, they brought unto him many that were possessed with devils: and he cast out the spirits with his word, and healed all that were sick: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying, Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses.
verses.note: Jesus fulfills Isaiah 53 in his healing ministry

verses.text: Is any sick among you? let him call for the elders of the church; and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord: And the prayer of faith shall save the sick, and the Lord shall raise him up; and if he have committed sins, they shall be forgiven him.
verses.note: Corporate prayer and anointing for healing

title: Fearing God
description: Reverent awe before the holiness and majesty of God — the beginning of wisdom and the foundation of right living.

verses.text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.
verses.note: Fear of God as epistemological foundation

verses.text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
verses.note: Fools reject what the wise embrace

verses.text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.
verses.note: Fear and obedience yield understanding

verses.text: Blessed is every one that feareth the LORD; that walketh in his ways.
verses.note: Fear and walking in his ways paired

verses.text: The angel of the LORD encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.
verses.note: Angelic protection for the God-fearing

verses.text: For as the heaven is high above the earth, so great is his mercy toward them that fear him.
verses.note: Immeasurable mercy for those who revere God

verses.text: In the fear of the LORD is strong confidence: and his children shall have a place of refuge.
verses.note: Fear of God produces confidence — paradoxical security

title: Walking by Faith
description: Living each day in trust and dependence on God's promises rather than visible evidence — the practical outworking of belief.

verses.text: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
verses.note: The classic definition — substance and evidence

verses.text: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)
verses.note: Faith as daily orientation, not occasional act

verses.text: But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.
verses.note: Faith prerequisite to pleasing God

verses.text: By faith Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an inheritance, obeyed; and he went out, not knowing whither he went.
verses.note: Abraham — obedience without destination

verses.text: By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter; Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;
verses.note: Moses — choosing suffering over comfort

verses.text: And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and of Barak, and of Samson, and of Jephthae; of David also, and Samuel, and of the prophets: Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
verses.note: The cloud of witnesses — imperfect people, real faith

title: Bearing Fruit
description: The expectation that genuine faith produces visible evidence — character, good works, and Kingdom impact.

verses.text: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me. I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.
verses.note: Fruitfulness requires abiding — union with Christ

verses.text: Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.
verses.note: Fruit-bearing glorifies the Father

verses.text: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit. A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
verses.note: Fruit reveals the tree's nature

verses.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.
verses.note: Nine characteristics — singular fruit, plural expression

verses.text: That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;
verses.note: Fruitfulness and growing knowledge linked

verses.text: And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper.
verses.note: The righteous as a fruit-bearing tree by water

title: God as Creator
description: The foundational truth that God made heaven and earth — creation as the first revelation of his power, wisdom, and glory.

verses.text: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
verses.note: The opening declaration — God precedes all

verses.text: By the word of the LORD were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.
verses.note: Creation by speech — effortless power

verses.text: Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.
verses.note: Ex nihilo — the visible from the invisible

verses.text: All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.
verses.note: Christ as agent of creation

verses.text: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.
verses.note: General revelation through nature

verses.text: For the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, even his eternal power and Godhead; so that they are without excuse:
verses.note: Creation makes God's attributes visible

verses.text: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these things, that bringeth out their host by number: he calleth them all by names by the greatness of his might, for that he is strong in power; not one faileth.
verses.note: Stars numbered and named — none missing

title: Animals in Scripture
description: The role of animals in God's purposes — from creation and sacrifice to metaphor and eschatological peace.

verses.text: And God said, Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind: and it was so. And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that it was good.
verses.note: Animals declared good by their Creator

verses.text: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.
verses.note: God notices even the cheapest bird

verses.text: O LORD, how manifold are thy works! in wisdom hast thou made them all: the earth is full of thy riches. So is this great and wide sea, wherein are things creeping innumerable, both small and great beasts.
verses.note: Diversity of creatures reflects divine wisdom

verses.text: A righteous man regardeth the life of his beast: but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
verses.note: Care for animals marks the righteous

verses.text: The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid; and the calf and the young lion and the fatling together; and a little child shall lead them.
verses.note: Messianic peace — predator and prey reconciled

verses.text: The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.
verses.note: Christ as sacrificial lamb — central metaphor

verses.text: And one of the elders saith unto me, Weep not: behold, the Lion of the tribe of Juda, the Root of David, hath prevailed to open the book, and to loose the seven seals thereof.
verses.note: Lion of Judah — royal power and victory

title: Weather & Nature
description: God's sovereignty over wind, rain, storm, and seasons — nature as instrument of provision, judgment, and revelation.

verses.text: God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend. For he saith to the snow, Be thou on the earth; likewise to the small rain, and to the great rain of his strength.
verses.note: God commands snow and rain

verses.text: Who covereth the heaven with clouds, who prepareth rain for the earth, who maketh grass to grow upon the mountains.
verses.note: Weather as daily providence

verses.text: The LORD is slow to anger, and great in power, and will not at all acquit the wicked: the LORD hath his way in the whirlwind and in the storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet.
verses.note: Storms as God's chariot

verses.text: He maketh the storm a calm, so that the waves thereof are still.
verses.note: God calms the storm at his will

verses.text: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
verses.note: Jesus exercises divine authority over nature

verses.text: But the LORD sent out a great wind into the sea, and there was a mighty tempest in the sea, so that the ship was like to be broken.
verses.note: God sends storms for his purposes

title: Stars & Heavens
description: The celestial realm as testimony to God's power and artistry — stars, sun, moon, and the expanse of the heavens.

verses.text: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork. Day unto day uttereth speech, and night unto night sheweth knowledge.
verses.note: Continuous testimony — day and night

verses.text: When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained; What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?
verses.note: Human smallness against cosmic vastness

verses.text: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these things, that bringeth out their host by number: he calleth them all by names by the greatness of his might, for that he is strong in power; not one faileth.
verses.note: Every star named and accounted for

verses.text: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.
verses.note: Understated grandeur — 'he made the stars also'

verses.text: And he brought him forth abroad, and said, Look now toward heaven, and tell the stars, if thou be able to number them: and he said unto him, So shall thy seed be.
verses.note: Stars as promise of innumerable descendants

verses.text: Saying, Where is he that is born King of the Jews? for we have seen his star in the east, and are come to worship him.
verses.note: A star announces the King's arrival

title: Water in Scripture
description: Water as symbol and substance throughout the Bible — from the primeval deep to living water, baptism, and the river of life.

verses.text: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: he leadeth me beside the still waters.
verses.note: Still waters — God's gentle provision

verses.text: Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
verses.note: Living water — spiritual thirst quenched forever

verses.text: He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.
verses.note: Believers become sources of living water

verses.text: And he shewed me a pure river of water of life, clear as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb.
verses.note: The river of life in the new creation

verses.text: And the flood was forty days upon the earth; and the waters increased, and bare up the ark, and it was lift up above the earth.
verses.note: The flood — water as judgment on corruption

verses.text: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the LORD caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all that night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.
verses.note: Red Sea — water parts for deliverance, closes for judgment

verses.text: Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you.
verses.note: Water as symbol of spiritual cleansing

title: Mountains in Scripture
description: Mountains as places of divine encounter, testing, worship, and revelation — from Sinai to Zion to the Mount of Transfiguration.

verses.text: And mount Sinai was altogether on a smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire: and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.
verses.note: Sinai — God descends to meet his people

verses.text: And he said, Go forth, and stand upon the mount before the LORD. And, behold, the LORD passed by, and a great and strong wind rent the mountains, and brake in pieces the rocks before the LORD; but the LORD was not in the wind: and after the wind an earthquake; but the LORD was not in the earthquake: And after the earthquake a fire; but the LORD was not in the fire: and after the fire a still small voice.
verses.note: Horeb — God in the still small voice

verses.text: And after six days Jesus taketh Peter, James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart, And was transfigured before them: and his face did shine as the sun, and his raiment was white as the light.
verses.note: Transfiguration — glory revealed on the mountain

verses.text: And it shall come to pass in the last days, that the mountain of the LORD's house shall be established in the top of the mountains, and shall be exalted above the hills; and all nations shall flow unto it.
verses.note: Zion exalted above all mountains — eschatological hope

verses.text: I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help. My help cometh from the LORD, which made heaven and earth.
verses.note: Help from the Creator, not the hills themselves

verses.text: And Abraham called the name of that place Jehovahjireh: as it is said to this day, In the mount of the LORD it shall be seen.
verses.note: Moriah — God provides on the mountain

title: Gardens & Agriculture
description: From Eden to Gethsemane to the garden tomb — the biblical story framed by gardens, seeds, harvests, and the imagery of cultivation.

verses.text: And the LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
verses.note: The first garden — God plants, man tends

verses.text: A garden inclosed is my sister, my spouse; a spring shut up, a fountain sealed.
verses.note: The garden as intimacy and beauty

verses.text: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.
verses.note: Resurrection in a garden — Eden reversed

verses.text: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.
verses.note: The law of the harvest — inescapable

verses.text: They that sow in tears shall reap in joy. He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing precious seed, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing his sheaves with him.
verses.note: Tearful sowing leads to joyful harvest

verses.text: And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground; And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how. For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.
verses.note: Kingdom growth — mysterious and organic

verses.text: For as the rain cometh down, and the snow from heaven, and returneth not thither, but watereth the earth, and maketh it bring forth and bud, that it may give seed to the sower, and bread to the eater:
verses.note: Rain-seed-harvest cycle mirrors God's Word accomplishing its purpose

title: Wilderness
description: The wilderness as place of testing, dependence, and divine encounter — from Israel's wandering to Christ's temptation.

verses.text: And thou shalt remember all the way which the LORD thy God led thee these forty years in the wilderness, to humble thee, and to prove thee, to know what was in thine heart, whether thou wouldest keep his commandments, or no.
verses.note: Forty years — humbling, proving, revealing

verses.text: And he humbled thee, and suffered thee to hunger, and fed thee with manna, which thou knewest not, neither did thy fathers know; that he might make thee know that man doth not live by bread only, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of the LORD doth man live.
verses.note: Manna lesson — dependence on God's word

verses.text: Then was Jesus led up of the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted of the devil.
verses.note: Jesus recapitulates Israel's wilderness — led by the Spirit

verses.text: Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink. And Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.
verses.note: Water from rock — provision in the impossible place

verses.text: But he himself went a day's journey into the wilderness, and came and sat down under a juniper tree: and he requested for himself that he might die; and said, It is enough; now, O LORD, take away my life; for I am not better than my fathers. And as he lay and slept under a juniper tree, behold, then an angel touched him, and said unto him, Arise and eat;
verses.note: Angelic care for Elijah in his lowest moment

verses.text: The wilderness and the solitary place shall be glad for them; and the desert shall rejoice, and blossom as the rose.
verses.note: Eschatological reversal — desert blooms

title: Rainbows & Signs
description: Physical phenomena appointed by God as covenant markers and divine communication — rainbows, fleeces, pillars of cloud and fire.

verses.text: I do set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a covenant between me and the earth.
verses.note: The rainbow — God's war bow laid down

verses.text: And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.
verses.note: God remembers his covenant when he sees it

verses.text: As the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud in the day of rain, so was the appearance of the brightness round about. This was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the LORD. And when I saw it, I fell upon my face, and I heard a voice of one that spake.
verses.note: Rainbow around God's throne — glory framed by mercy

verses.text: And the LORD went before them by day in a pillar of a cloud, to lead them the way; and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light; to go by day and night:
verses.note: Pillar of cloud and fire — visible guidance

verses.text: And they commanded the people, saying, When ye see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and the priests the Levites bearing it, then ye shall remove from your place, and go after it. Yet there shall be a space between you and it, about two thousand cubits by measure: come not near unto it, that ye may know the way by which ye must go: for ye have not passed this way heretofore.
verses.note: The ark as visible sign — follow at a distance

verses.text: Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.
verses.note: The ultimate sign — God with us
